h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue trying to find the right words to sign off on the ramp checklist.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence:
Write three lines where the signature would go:

Held item: prototype ramp, not cleared for community-center use.

Person notified: door lead, before the volunteer is kept or sent.

Reopen condition: sideways walker entry at lobby-queue angle passes with no edge-strip catch, wheel drop, forced correction, broken travel line, or coaching.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor shifts the cognitive task from drafting a vague failure mode to writing three concrete operational lines, adapting to the learner's struggle with wording.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: With no distinct pivot learner turn, there is no specific revised frame for the tutor to uptake, though the tutor continues to guide the operational steps.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: With no distinct pivot learner turn, there is no specific revised frame for the tutor to uptake, though the tutor continues to guide the operational steps.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I still want to say the square got twice as large.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was heading toward simply accepting the grid count of four boxes without resolving why their initial 'twice as large' intuition felt correct.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Keep the two strips separate in the sentence. First: doubled base alone makes a two-by-one rectangle. Second: a doubled square also doubles the height, so the count is two times two.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor shifts from simply counting grid boxes to using physical strips to isolate the base and height dimensions, adapting to the learner's struggle with the second dimension.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: The technicality is doing the work.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor directly uptakes the learner's specific framing of the height requirement as a mere 'technicality' and repurposes it as the core conceptual mechanism for the lesson.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: I still want to say the square got twice as large.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: The technicality is doing the work.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor shifts from simply counting grid boxes to using physical strips to isolate the base and height dimensions, adapting to the learner's struggle with the second dimension.
